definition: The CMA (conservative-minus-aggressive) factor is ~3 years into a potential 5-7 year sign flip cycle where aggressive investment outperforms conservative. Self-funded booms persist longer. Historical mean reversion produces 3-5 sigma losses within 12 months. Monitoring metric: capex/OCF crossing 55-65% for 2-3 hyperscalers (projected late 2027).
evidence: CMA returned -200 to -400bp in 1994-2000, -150 to -300bp in 2010-2014, -200 to -350bp currently
2000-2001 reversal: +25% cumulative CMA return within 18 months
Self-funded booms persist longer due to no financing constraint
open_questions: Whether capex/OCF threshold is the right trigger indicator
Small-n problem limits precision on timing and magnitude
